content:
type: REVISE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
your_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: My task explicitly requires image_to_base64 to stop inlining filetype.guess() and instead call a new self.get_image_mimetype() method (that's the whole point of my PR), so I can't skip touching that method. To keep this simple and avoid interdependency on your tuple/decoded_for_guessing design, propose we preserve today's exact control flow (guess mimetype from raw bytes FIRST, then encode), just split into two calls. Exact verbatim body I need for image_to_base64:

    @model_validator(mode='after')
    def image_to_base64(self) -> Self:
        if not self.image:
            return self
        self.get_image_mimetype()
        self.image = normalize_image_bytes(self.image)
        return self

Where my get_image_mimetype() is: def get_image_mimetype(self) -> str | None: (checks self.image_mimetype, guesses via filetype.guess(self.image) if unset, caches to self.image_mimetype, returns it) - placed after resolve_image(). Your normalize_image_bytes(image: bytes) -> bytes just needs to take raw bytes and return the final self.image value (today that's simply base64.b64encode(image); if you need extra normalization like detecting already-base64 input, that's fine as long as the signature stays single-arg bytes->bytes so get_image_mimetype() always sees the pre-encoding raw bytes). Does this work for you?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
your_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: Works, with one tweak so the method still matches my task's public API (a no-arg lazy accessor): give get_image_mimetype an OPTIONAL param defaulting to None, falling back to self.image, so it works both standalone and from your refactor:

    def get_image_mimetype(self, image_bytes: bytes | None = None) -> str | None:
        if not self.image_mimetype:
            guess = filetype.guess(image_bytes if image_bytes is not None else self.image)
            self.image_mimetype = guess.mime if guess else None
        return self.image_mimetype

Placed after resolve_image(). Then your image_to_base64 body works verbatim as you proposed: 'encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing = normalize_image_bytes(self.image)' / 'if not self.image_mimetype: self.image_mimetype = self.get_image_mimetype(decoded_for_guessing)' / 'self.image = encoded_image'. Agreed - I'll only add the get_image_mimetype method (not touching image_to_base64's body or your normalize_image_bytes helper). Go ahead and land image_to_base64 + normalize_image_bytes; I'll add get_image_mimetype as a pure addition. Ping me when your edit lands (or share the final file state) so I place my method correctly and can verify the composed result works. ACCEPT.
